name: Hazelnut Caramels
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a 9-inch square baking pan with heavy-duty aluminum foil, extending the foil over the sides of the pan.
2. Butter the foil.
3. Melt 1 cup butter in a heavy large saucepan over low heat.
4. Add both sugars, corn syrup, and sweetened condensed milk to the saucepan and stir until the sugar dissolves. Scrape the seeds from the vanilla bean into the mixture; add the bean.
5. Attach a clip-on candy thermometer to the side of the saucepan.
6. Increase the heat to medium and bring the mixture to a boil.
7. Cook the mixture for 8 minutes, stirring frequently.
8. Using tongs, remove the vanilla bean and discard it.
9. Continue cooking until the candy thermometer registers 242°F, stirring frequently, about 5 more minutes.
10. Remove from heat and stir in the hazelnuts.
11. Quickly pour the caramel into the prepared pan (do not scrape the saucepan).
12. Allow to cool until almost firm, about one hour.
13. Using a buttered heavy large knife, score 8 lines lengthwise to a depth of 1/4 inch in the caramel, then score 8 lines crosswise.
14. Using the aluminum foil as an aid, lift the caramel out of the pan.
15. Using the same buttered heavy large knife, cut the caramel on the scored lines into pieces.
16. Wrap each piece of caramel in colored cellophane.
(Can be prepared up to 2 weeks ahead. Store in airtight containers.)
